note:
Mozilla处于绝对的状态：高管理费用，Firefox的使用率下降，收入来源可疑，现在随着收入的下降，他们在工程上进行了大幅削减。
Mozilla最近宣布他们将解雇250人。这是他们劳动力的四分之一，因此他们的工作也有一些大幅裁员。受害者包括：MDN文档(那些是每个人都比W3School更喜欢的网络标准文档)，Rust编译器，甚至对Firefox开发的一些削减。像大多数人一样，我希望看到Mozilla做得很好，但这三个项目几乎就是我认为Mozilla的全部意义所在，所以这个消息是一个很大的失望。
声明的削减原因是收入下降。Mozilla的资金很大程度上依赖于版税。作为付费的回报，Mozilla允许大型科技公司选择Firefox的默认搜索引擎-这些科技公司最终付费是为了增加Firefox用户使用它们进行的搜索数量。除了将原因归咎于冠状病毒外，Mozilla Haven对这些版税降低的原因并不是特别透明。
我相信冠状病毒帮不了太大的忙，但我怀疑更大的问题是，火狐的市场份额现在只有以前的一小部分，因此版税也会更少--用户更少，搜索也更少，因此给Mozilla的钱也更少。
然而，真正的问题并不是特许权使用费的削减。Mozilla已经收到了足够多的钱，可以让自己实现经济独立。Mozilla每年(每年)获得高达5亿美元的收入。多年来。真正的问题是，Mozilla并没有用这笔钱来实现财务独立，而是每年都花掉它，在组织上相当于勉强糊口。
尽管作为一个拥有营利性组织的非营利性组织，Mozilla的法律结构略有做作，但它和其他任何一个非政府组织一样，都是一个非政府组织。在这篇文章中，我想把适用于其他非政府组织的传统方法应用到Mozilla上，以表明哪里出了问题。
评估一家非政府组织最受欢迎、最直观的方法之一，就是判断它们的工作计划(或使命)花了多少钱，还有多少花在了其他事情上，比如行政和筹款。如果你把钱捐给一家慈善机构，为第三世界的人们提供食物，你希望你给他们的大部分钱都花在食物上--而不是，例如，为总部员工购买公司汽车。
从这个角度来看，Mozilla看起来很糟糕。全部支出中有整整30%用于行政管理。慈善领航者，一个衡量非政府组织有效性的组织，会在相关指标上给他们打零分(满分10分)。就背景而言，要达到这一标准的5/10，Mozilla管理需要低于支出的25%，而对于10/10，则需要低于15%。
高级管理人员也为自己做得非常好。Mozilla首席执行官米切尔·贝克(Mitchell Baker)2018年的薪酬为240万美元，我个人认为这是一笔瞬间的代际财富。在过去的五年里，支付给贝克的款项增加了一倍多。
据我所知，没有一家总部设在英国的非政府组织的高管年收入超过100万英镑(合130万美元)。英国当然也有相当一部分大型国际非政府组织--其中许多比Mozilla大得多，也更重要。
我意识到有些人不喜欢将管理费用作为一种衡量标准，并认为行政开支有可能提高效率。我认为很难说Mozilla的管理费用与效率的提高有关。
现在，Mozilla不再认为自己是旧网景套件的保管人，而更多地将自己视为隐私非政府组织。Mozilla内部的一个口号是：超越浏览器。
不管他们如何看待自己，他们的大部分收入来自帮助引导流量到谷歌，使该搜索引擎成为Firefox的默认搜索引擎。谷歌通过一个大型的有针对性的广告系统从这些流量中赚钱，该系统在很大程度上是在没有得到人们同意的情况下通过网络跟踪人们的。事实上，这一收入下降的原因之一是，随着火狐使用量的下降，谷歌的流量减少了，因此谷歌支付的费用也会更低。
到目前为止，道德哲学家们还没有就普遍的道德准则达成一致。然而，我认为大多数人都会认识到Mozilla与谷歌的关系是虚伪的。除了伦理问题之外，这种关系似乎肯定会产生利益冲突。任何人都会认为，隐私非政府组织会从一开始就在他们的浏览器中建立反跟踪对策。事实上，这是在苹果(2017年)和Brave(自发布以来)都铺平了道路之后，相对较新的(2019年)才增加的。显然，在火狐没有反跟踪功能的情况下，Mozilla作为谷歌附庸的地位在很长一段时间里都起到了一定的作用。
另一个道德问题是Mozilla进军VPN的重大新举措。从隐私的角度来看，这没有多大意义。广义地说：VPN对于浏览网页的人来说并不是一个有用的隐私工具。VPN允许您通过代理访问互联网-因此，您的请求表面上看起来似乎来自其他地方，但实际上并不是这样。这无助于解决网络用户的主要隐私问题：他们正被谷歌和其他地方的坏人大规模被动跟踪和去匿名化。无论IP地址如何，此跟踪都会发生。
当我通过Mozilla VPN(穆拉瓦德VPN的更名)测试Firefox时，我发现我可以通过浏览器指纹识别来去匿名化--这已经是一种相当广泛的技术，可以通过检查浏览器的各种元素来生成指纹，然后再用它来重新识别你。与其他一些浏览器不同，Firefox不包含任何针对此的对策。
另一个令人担忧的问题是，许多关注隐私的VPN服务都有一个令人讨厌的习惯，那就是记录用户行为的大量日志。几个月前，几家没有日志记录的VPN服务无意中泄露了他们承诺不会在大规模入侵中收集的TB级私人用户数据。VPN服务处于很好的窃听位置-即使它们承诺不会，您唯一的选择就是信守诺言。
我已经讨论过Mozilla主席令人印象深刻的薪酬：240万美元/年。莫兹拉取得了同样令人印象深刻的业绩，难道这样令人印象深刻的薪酬是合理的吗？可悲的是，几乎在所有定量和定性的结果衡量标准上，Mozilla都是一条狗。
火狐现在是如此的小众，以至于有可能获得狂热的追随者：它的市场份额只有4%，低于10年前的30%。手机浏览数据惨淡：火狐几乎不存在于手机上，市场份额不到0.5%。这令人费解，因为移动火狐浏览器有一个罕见的功能：它可以安装扩展，因此可以拦截广告。
然而，尽管核心业务存在问题，Mozilla非但没有缩减开支，反而迅速实现了多元化。近年来，Mozilla创建了：
遗憾的是，Mozilla的年度报告没有按项目细分支出，因此我们不可能知道在Mozilla项目上的支出中有多少花在了Firefox上，又有多少花在了所有这些附带项目上。
你至少可以推断出副业是昂贵的。软件开发总是如此。上面提到的每个项目(以及所有其他从未宣布或我不知道的项目)都需要业务分析师、设计师、用户研究人员、开发人员、测试人员以及您创建消费者网络项目所需的所有其他人员。
当然，最大的成本是仅仅将这笔钱花在其他东西上的机会成本-或者什么都不花：它本可以投资于建立捐赠基金。现在，Mozilla的处境显然是没有足够的钱来完全资助Firefox的开发。
莫兹拉不能再像以前那样继续下去了。至少，他们需要减少开支，以配合他们现在减少的收入。不过，这笔收入可能仍然相当巨大：每年可能有数亿美元。
我是一名Firefox用户(显然，我也是为数不多的移动用户之一)，我希望看到Mozilla取得成功。因此，我希望Mozilla能够降低他们的管理成本。我还希望他们增加在火狐上的花费，让它更快，并实现其他浏览器拥有的那些隐私功能。最重要的是：我希望他们开始建立适当的财务独立。
我怀疑这些事情会不会发生。相反，他们可能会保留昂贵的管理。他们已经削减了火狐的开支。他们最大的希望是继续尝试新事物，比如使用他们的品牌来销售VPN服务，正如我已经讨论过的，这些服务并不能解决他们用户的问题。
Mozilla应该向他们的用户索要钱，而不是分散到更多的产品和服务上。多年来，“卫报”(一家规模与Mozilla类似的机构，就员工规模而言)在财务上一文不名。几年前，“卫报”开始向读者索要资金，从那时起，英国“卫报”的财务基础似乎更加稳固。
直接获得资金也有助于使他们组织的激励措施与读者的激励措施保持一致。也许这会对Mozilla起作用。但话说回来，“卫报”的情况就不同了。他们的首席执行官一年只赚36万英磅。
请随时给我发一封关于这篇文章的电子邮件，特别是如果你不同意这篇文章的话。
我大约每月写一篇新文章。当我写了一些新东西时，你可以通过电子邮件提醒或RSS提要得到通知。
另一个花费巨大的著名科技非政府组织是维基媒体基金会(Wikimedia Foundation)。你可以在维基百科上读到更多关于他们问题的内容，“癌症”使用了一个非常狡猾的比喻，但除此之外，它是一个很好的关于令人难以置信的支出增长的研究。与Mozilla相比，Wikimedia有一个优势，即其主要项目的所有贡献者都是无偿的-这只会让费用变得更加令人兴奋。和Mozilla一样，Wikimedia也有很多附带项目。他们一度秘密地试图建立一个全新的搜索引擎。
儿童公司(Kids Company)是一个很好的慈善机构，它是一家帮助弱势儿童的英国慈善机构，由于费用控制不力而关闭。Kids Company的崩溃表明，停摆可能是相当突然的。英国政府提供了大部分资金，但(最终)失去了信心，停止了他们的年度赠款，导致该慈善机构立即被清算。下议院的一个委员会发布了一份不错的报告。
作为一家拥有营利性部门的非营利组织，Mozilla的法律结构使得他们的政府文件(表格990；s)用途相当有限，因为它们没有合并，也不包含关于Mozilla大部分活动的任何信息。这些文件最有用的一点是，母公司董事会中的人必须披露补贴的薪酬(在第九部分，一般在第10页)。ProPublica有一整套。
更有用的是Mozilla合并后的年度报告。我还没能找到这些的主索引，但你可以通过Mozilla的状态页面找到-你需要编辑url中的年份才能得到以前的年度报告。
我使用的是StatCounter的使用数据。我还没有包括任何2020年的数据，因为这一年还没有出来，但对火狐来说，这比2019年还要糟糕。
note_en:
Mozilla is in an absolute state: high overheads, falling usage of Firefox, questionable sources of revenue and now making big cuts to engineering as their income falls.
      Mozilla recently announced that they would be dismissing 250 people. That's a quarter of their workforce so there are some deep cuts to their work too. The victims include: the MDN docs (those are the web standards docs everyone likes better than w3schools), the Rust compiler and even some cuts to Firefox development. Like most people I want to see Mozilla do well but those three projects comprise pretty much what I think of as the whole point of Mozilla, so this news is a a big let down.
  The stated reason for the cuts is falling income. Mozilla largely relies on "royalties" for funding. In return for payment, Mozilla allows big technology companies to choose the default search engine in Firefox - the technology companies are ultimately paying to increase the number of searches Firefox users make with them. Mozilla haven't been particularly transparent about why these royalties are being reduced, except to blame the coronavirus.
  I'm sure the coronavirus is not a great help but I suspect the bigger problem is that Firefox's market share is now a tiny fraction of its previous size and so the royalties will be smaller too - fewer users, so fewer searches and therefore less money for Mozilla.
  The real problem is not the royalty cuts, though. Mozilla has already received more than enough money to set themselves up for financial independence. Mozilla received up to half a billion dollars a year (each year!) for many years. The  real problem is that Mozilla didn't use that money to achieve financial independence and instead just spent it each year, doing the organisational equivalent of living hand-to-mouth.
  Despite their slightly contrived legal structure as a non-profit that owns a for-profit, Mozilla are an NGO just like any other. In this article I want to apply the traditional measures that are applied to other NGOs to Mozilla in order to show what's wrong.
      One of the most popular and most intuitive ways to evaluate an NGO is to judge how much of their spending is on their programme of works (or "mission") and how much is on other things, like administration and fundraising. If you give money to a charity for feeding people in the third world you hope that most of the money you give them goes on food - and not, for example, on company cars for head office staff.
  Mozilla looks bad when considered in this light. Fully 30% of all expenditure goes on administration. Charity Navigator, an organisation that measures NGO effectiveness, would give them   zero out of ten on the relevant metric. For context, to achieve 5/10 on that measure Mozilla admin would need to be under 25% of spending and, for 10/10, under 15%.
  Senior executives have also done very well for themselves. Mitchell Baker, Mozilla's top executive, was paid $2.4m in 2018, a sum I personally think of as instant inter-generational wealth. Payments to Baker have more than doubled in the last five years.
  As far as I can find, there is no UK-based NGO whose top executive makes more than £1m ($1.3m) a year. The UK certainly has its fair share of big international NGOs - many much bigger and more significant than Mozilla.
  I'm aware that   some people dislike overheads as a measure and argue that it's possible for administration spending can increase effectiveness. I think it's hard to argue that Mozilla's overheads are correlated with an improvement in effectiveness.
    Mozilla now thinks of itself less as a custodian of the old Netscape suite and more as a 'privacy NGO'. One slogan inside Mozilla is: "Beyond the Browser".
  Regardless of how they view themselves, most of their income comes from helping to direct traffic to Google by making that search engine the default in Firefox. Google make money off that traffic via a big targeted advertising system that tracks people across the web and largely without their consent. Indeed, one of the reasons this income is falling is because as Firefox's usage falls less traffic is being directed Google's way and so Google will pay less.
  There is, as yet, no outbreak of agreement among the moral philosophers as to a universal code of ethics. However I think most people would recognise hypocrisy in Mozilla's relationship with Google. Beyond the ethical problems, the relationship certainly seems to create conflicts of interest. Anyone would think that a privacy NGO would build anti-tracking countermeasures into their browser right from the start. In fact, this was only added relatively recently ( in 2019), after both Apple ( in 2017) and Brave (since release) paved the way. It certainly seems like Mozilla's status as a Google vassal has played a role in the absence of anti-tracking features in Firefox for so long.
  Another ethical issue is Mozilla's big new initiative to  move into VPNs. This doesn't make a lot of sense from a privacy point of view. Broadly speaking: VPNs are not a useful privacy tool for people browsing the web. A VPN lets you access the internet through a proxy - so your requests superficially appear to come from somewhere other than they really do. This does nothing to address the main privacy problem for web users: that they are being passively tracked and de-anonymised on a massive scale by the baddies at Google and elsewhere. This tracking happens regardless of IP address.
  When I tested Firefox through  Mozilla VPN (a rebrand of  Mullavad VPN) I found that I could be de-anonymised by browser fingerprinting - already a fairly widespread technique by which various elements of your browser are examined to create a "fingerprint" which can then be used to re-identify you later. Firefox, unlike some other browsers, does not include any countermeasures against this.
    Another worry is that many of these privacy focused VPN services have a nasty habit of turning out to keep copious logs on user behaviour. A few months ago several "no log" VPN services inadvertently released terabytes of private user data that they had promised not to collect  in a massive breach. VPN services are in a great position to eavesdrop - and even if they promise not to, your only option is to take them at their word.
    I've discussed the Mozilla chair's impressive pay: $2.4m/year. Surely such impressive pay is justified by the equally impressive results Mozilla has achieved? Sadly on almost every measure of results both quantitative and qualitative, Mozilla is a dog.
  Firefox is now so niche it is in danger of garnering a cult following: it has just 4% market share, down from 30% a decade ago. Mobile browsing numbers are bleak: Firefox barely exists on phones, with a market share of less than half a percent. This is baffling given that mobile Firefox has a rare feature for a mobile browser: it's able to install extensions and so can block ads.
  Yet despite the problems within their core business, Mozilla, instead of retrenching, has diversified rapidly. In recent years Mozilla has created:
      Sadly  Mozilla's annual report doesn't break down expenses on a per-project basis so it's impossible to know how much of the spending that  is on Mozilla's programme is being spent on Firefox and how much is being spent on all these other side-projects.
  What you can at least infer is that the side-projects are expensive. Software development always is. Each of the projects named above (and all the other ones that were never announced or that I don't know about) will have required business analysts, designers, user researchers, developers, testers and all the other people you need in order to create a consumer web project.
  The biggest cost of course is the opportunity cost of just spending that money on other stuff - or nothing: it could have been invested to build an endowment. Now Mozilla is in the situation where apparently there isn't enough money left to fully fund Firefox development.
    Mozilla can't just continue as before. At the very least they need to reduce their expenses to go along with their now reduced income. That income is probably still pretty enormous though: likely hundreds of millions a year.
  I'm a Firefox user (and one of the few on mobile, apparently) and I want to see Mozilla succeed. As such, I would hope that Mozilla would cut their cost of administration. I'd also hope that they'd increase spending on Firefox to make it faster and implement those privacy features that other browsers have. Most importantly: I'd like them to start building proper financial independence.
  I doubt those things will happen. Instead they will likely keep the expensive management. They have already cut spending on Firefox. Their great hope is to continue trying new things, like using their brand to sell VPN services that, as I've discussed, do not solve the problem that their users have.
  Instead of diversifying into yet more products and services Mozilla should probably just ask their users for money. For many years the Guardian newspaper (a similarly sized organisation to Mozilla in terms of staff) was a financial basket case. The Guardian started  asking their readers for money a few years ago and seems to be on firmer financial footing since.
  Getting money directly has also helped align the incentives of their organisation with those of their readers. Perhaps that would work for Mozilla. But then, things are different at the Guardian. Their chief exec makes a mere £360,000 a year.
    Please do feel free to send me  an email about this article, especially if you disagreed with it.
    I write a new article roughly once a month. You can get notified when I write something new  by email alert or  by RSS feed.
      Another notable technology NGO with huge expenses is The Wikimedia foundation. You can read more about their problems in  Wikipedia has Cancer which uses a very dodgy metaphor but is otherwise a good study of incredible spending growth there. Wikimedia has the advantage over Mozilla that all contributors to its main project are unpaid - which only makes the expenses all the more mindblowing. Wikimedia, like Mozilla, has had a lot of side projects. At one point they were trying, in secret, to build a  whole new search engine.
  A good example of a charity that shut down due to poor expense control is  Kids Company, a British charity for helping vulnerable children. The Kids Company debacle shows that shutdown can be quite abrupt. The British government provided most of the funding but (eventually) lost faith and ceased their annual grant which resulted in the charity being immediately liquidated. A House of Commons committee released   a good report.
    Mozilla's legal structure as a non-profit that owns a for-profit arm makes their government filings ("Form 990"s) of pretty limited use as they aren't  consolidated and don't contain any information about the majority of Mozilla activities. The most useful thing about these documents is that people who are on the board of the parent have to disclose pay from the subsidary (in Part IX, generally on page 10).  Propublica have a complete set.
  More useful is Mozilla's annual report, which is consolidated. I haven't been able to find a master index of these but you can get there via the  "State of Mozilla" pages - you'll need to edit the year in the url to get previous annual reports.
  I used   statcounter's usage data. I haven't included any 2020 data as the year isn't out yet but it's even worse than 2019 for Firefox.
title: 尽管Mozilla的最高高管薪酬上涨了400%，但Firefox的使用率下降了85%
title_en: Firefox usage is down 85% despite Mozilla's top exec pay going up 400%

note:
在上周三思而后行之后，我想这周我应该谈一谈看起来(但不是)相反的事情：半途而废(如果你说美式英语，那就是半途而废)。
半途而废是指你投入的努力比获得好结果所需的努力要少，而你从中得到的结果也相应地更差。这是拼凑起来的，偷工减料，任何你没有做得更好的事情，不是因为你做不到，只是因为你不能被打扰。
就像想得太多一样，实际上，半途而废是好的，而且得到了不公平的坏名声，你可能应该做得比你现在做得更多。
你为什么要做半个屁眼的事情？一般说来，这是因为要做好这件事比任务所允许的要难得多。
大多数需要做的事情并不需要做得很好。如果另一种选择根本做不到他们，他们往往不需要做得很好。我们通常只有那么多的时间和精力可以花在一件事上，做这件事总比不做好。
一般来说，你的目标应该是在任何给定的事情上投入与结果成比例的努力。隐藏起来的东西可以用一件丑陋的杂物来做。一些对项目的成功没有意义的东西可以省略。
特别是当有很多事情要做，而要花费的精力有限的时候，这一点就会出现。在这种情况下，拒绝半个屁股实际上不是拒绝半个屁股，而是放弃对哪个比特到半个屁股的责任。帕金森琐碎定律就是一个很好的例子，比如一家核电站的委员会花了不成比例的时间来决定自行车棚的颜色。你应该对这个决定半途而废，基本上没问题。
有时甚至不可能在你可用的手段内解决问题。您可能会被禁止，或者必须在其他人设置的限制下工作(参见。散布指责)，或者你可能会选择一个完全假设的例子，因为你写时事通讯的时间太晚了，想去伙伴岛上的烧烤会见你的新邻居(不过，我保证，我们不会超过6个人，英国政府的🤞)。在许多这样的情况下，即使不是理想的情况，做一些改善情况的事情仍然是值得的。
这就是为什么半途而废是好的，但为什么人们做得还不够多呢？
嗯，就我个人而言，我责怪学校(我试着不用这种方式解释太多事情，但我认为这件事真的很有道理)。我们成长过程中的太多时间都是在这样一个环境中度过的，在这种环境中，有一个外部仲裁者会惩罚我们，因为我们做得不“适当”，而且不允许我们使用自己的判断来判断什么是足够好的。
太多时候，这会导致我们获得不适当程度的完美主义，我们唯一的半自命不凡的选择是要么说服自己事情不重要，要么拖延到我们别无选择，只能做半个屁股才能按时完成事情。
我们也可能最终通过根本不做事情来“解决”这个问题，因为我们从来没有时间做好它，因为我们完全知道它不值得做好。我发现人们是在大量阅读的情况下这样做的(这一点我特别归咎于学校)。我昨天读了一本书，从头到尾，大约一个小时。我的意思是，我读开头，读结尾，我读中间的一些页面，但可能不到总内容的10%，跳过所有无聊或恼人的部分。
为什么？嗯，因为它不是一本很好的书，也不值更多的钱，但里面有足够有趣的内容，我很重视这本书的意义，它绝对值一个小时，但它可能不值两个小时，而且我不可能在不到四个小时的时间里“正确”地读完它。如果我坚持要好好读这本书，我根本就不会读这本书，而且我也会因此而变得更糟。
大多数人不愿意以这种方式阅读，因为如果他们阅读不当，他们会有一位看不见的老师坐在他们的肩膀上评判他们(参见。吉米尼·克里克特(吉米尼·板球必须死)。除了阻止他们不恰当地阅读，这也会阻止他们“正确”地阅读，因为如果你不能读完一本书，那么开始阅读注定会让你要么花太多的时间在它值得的地方，要么为你读不到的“失败”而感到难过。
最终，掌握半壁江山的技能可能不会以这种方式提高你的工作质量，因为它可以让你有效地确定优先顺序，并判断一个项目实际值得的质量水平。通过纠正我们不适当的完美主义水平，我们可以将适当的努力投入到真正重要的问题上，而不会被那些不重要的无意义的部分所困扰。
note_en:
After  overthinking overthinking last week I thought this week I’d talk about something that seems like (but isn’t) its opposite: half-arsing (half-assing if you speak US English).
 Half-arsing is when you put in less effort than would be needed to get a good result, and the result you get out of it is correspondingly worse. It’s the kludge, the cutting of corners, anything where where the reason you didn’t do better is not that you couldn’t, just that you couldn’t be bothered.
 Like overthinking, half-arsing is good, actually, and gets an unfairly bad rep, and you should probably be doing more of it than you are.
 Why should you half-arse things? Generally because it’s harder to do it properly than the task warrants.
 Most things that need doing  don’t need doing very well. They  especially often don’t need doing well if the alternative is not doing them at all. Often we have only so much time and effort we can spend on a thing, and doing the thing is better than not doing the thing.
 In general, you should aim to put effort into any given thing proportionate to outcome. Something that is hidden away can be done with an ugly kludge. Something that does not meaningfully contribute to the success of the project can be skimped on.
 This comes up particularly when there are many things to do and a finite amount of effort to spend. In these case a refusal to half-arse is not actually a refusal to half-arse, it’s abdicating responsibility over which bits to half-arse.  Parkinson’s law of triviality, illustrated with the example that a committee for a nuclear power plant spends a disproportionate amount of time deciding on the colour of its bike shed, is essentially an example of this. You should probably just half-arse that decision, it’s basically fine.
 Sometimes it’s even impossible to solve the problem within the means available to you. You might be prohibited, or having to work in the constraints set by other people (cf.  Distributing Blame), or you might  to pick a completely hypothetical example have left writing your newsletter too late and want to head over to the barbecue on your partners’ island to meet your new neighbours (no more than six of us though, I promise, UK government 🤞). In many of these cases, it’s still worth doing something that improves the situation even if it’s not the ideal scenario.
 So that’s why half-arsing is good, but why do people not do enough of it?
 Well, personally I blame school (I try not to explain  too many things this way, but I think this one really makes sense). We spend too much of our formative years in an environment where there is an external arbiter who will punish us for not doing the thing “properly”, and does not allow us to use our judgement as to what counts as good enough.
 Too often this causes us to acquire an inappropriate degree of perfectionism, where our only options for half-arsery are to either convince ourselves that the thing is unimportant, or we procrastinate until we have no choice but to half-arse in order to get things done in time.
 We may also end up “solving” this problem by never doing the thing at all because we never make time to do it properly because we know full well it’s not worth doing properly. I find people do this with reading a lot (I  especially blame school for this one). I read a book yesterday, start to finish, in about an hour. By which I mean I read the start, I read the finish, and I read some of the pages in between but probably fewer than 10% of the total content, by skipping over all of the bits that were boring or annoying.
 Why? Well because it wasn’t a very good book, and it wasn’t worth more than that, but it had enough interesting content in it and I valued the sense of the book enough that it was definitely worth an hour, but it probably wasn’t worth two, and I couldn’t have read it “properly” in less than about four. If I’d insisted on a proper reading of it, I’d never have read the book at all, and I’d have been the worse for it.
 Most people are not willing to read this way, because they have an invisible teacher sitting on their shoulder judging them if they read improperly (cf.  Jiminy Cricket Must Die). As well as preventing them from reading improperly this also prevents them from reading “properly”, because if you cannot half-arse a book then starting reading it dooms you to either probably spending too much time on it for what it’s worth, or to feeling bad about your “failure” to read it.
 Ultimately, mastering the skill of half-arsery will probably net  improve the quality of your work in this way, because it allows you to prioritise effectively, and to judge what level of quality a project actually deserved. By fixing our inappropriate levels of perfectionism, we can invest the appropriate amount of effort into the problems that actually matter, and not get caught up on the pointless bits that don’t.
title: 为保护自己的半个世纪而战

note:
华盛顿-9月9日，美国空军高级采购官员向国防新闻证实，美国空军已经秘密设计、制造和飞行了至少一架神秘的下一代战斗机原型。14.。
这一进展肯定会震惊国防社区，他们上一次看到实验战斗机的首次飞行是在20年前联合打击战斗机合同的战斗中。由于空军未来的战斗机计划仍处于初级阶段，预计示威者的首次亮相和成功首飞在几年内都是不可能的。
威尔·罗珀(Will Roper)在空军协会航空、空间和网络会议之前接受国防新闻专访时表示：“我们已经在现实世界中建造并飞行了一个全尺寸的飞行演示器，我们在这样做的过程中打破了纪录。”“我们已经准备好以前所未有的方式制造下一代飞机。”
由于下一代空中优势计划的分类，关于飞机本身的几乎每一个细节都将是一个谜。下一代空中优势计划是空军为部署一系列联网的空战系统所做的努力，其中可能包括战斗机、无人机和其他太空或网络领域的联网平台。
罗珀拒绝评论已经试飞了多少架原型飞机，或者是哪些国防承包商制造了这些飞机。他不愿透露第一次飞行的时间和地点。他拒绝透露飞机设计的任何方面-它的任务，无论是无人驾驶还是随意配备人员，是否可以高超音速飞行，或者是否具有隐形特征。
罗珀说，重要的是，就在空军完成了对替代方案的分析一年后，空军已经证明，它可以使用尖端的先进制造技术来制造和测试下一架战斗机的虚拟版本-然后着手建造一个全尺寸的原型，并将其与机上的任务系统一起飞行。
罗珀说，“这不仅仅是你可以应用于简单系统的东西”，比如波音公司的T-7红鹰教练机，这是第一架使用数字工程、敏捷软件开发和开放架构“三位一体”制造的空军飞机。
“我们正在寻找有史以来建造的最复杂的系统，并用这种数字技术检查了所有的盒子。事实上，(我们)不仅检查了方框，(我们)还展示了一些真正神奇的东西。“
现在，下一代空中优势计划(NGAD)正处于决定点。罗珀拒绝透露空军将其下一代战斗机投入生产的速度，只说“相当快”。但在该部门决定开始生产新一代战斗机之前，它必须确定将承诺购买多少架飞机，以及希望何时开始购买-所有这些选择都可能影响2022财年的预算。
该计划本身有可能从根本上撼动国防工业。如果空军在短期内采取行动购买NGAD，它将为F-35和F-15EX项目增加一个挑战者，可能会将这些项目置于风险之中。
由于制造NGAD至关重要的先进制造技术是由商业部门首创的，该项目可能会为新的主承包商的出现打开大门-或许还会给SpaceX创始人埃隆·马斯克(Elon Musk)设计F-35竞争对手的机会。
罗珀说：“我不得不想象，将会有很多工程师--也许是拥有数十亿美元可投资的家喻户晓的名人--决定创办世界上最伟大的飞机公司，与空军一起制造世界上最伟大的飞机，这正是他们想要做的那种鼓舞人心的事情，作为一项业余爱好，甚至是一项主要的工作。”
美国企业研究所(American Enterprise Institute)国防预算分析师麦肯齐·伊格伦(Mackenzie Eaglen)表示，在空军面临预算限制、需要获得势头的关键时期，披露飞行中的全尺寸战斗机原型可能正是空军从国会获得更多财政支持所需的。
她说：“如果你能迅速取得进展，并通过产品展示进展，这只会改变国会山的整个动态。”“(罗珀)遇到了如此多的阻力，似乎这将是他的想法在概念上取得成功的一个可能途径。”
驾驶其未来战斗机的原型是容易的部分。现在空军必须选择是否承诺采取激进的购买方法。
在过去的50年里，美国的工业基础已经从10家有能力制造先进战斗机的制造商减少到只有3家国防公司：洛克希德·马丁公司(Lockheed Martin)、波音公司(Boeing)和诺斯罗普·格鲁曼公司(Northrop Grumman)。空军将一架新型战斗机从研发转移到全速生产所需的时间从几年到几十年不等。
其结果是，每个战斗机项目对公司来说都变得事关生死存亡，他们争先恐后地证明，他们可以比竞争对手更低的成本满足开发和生产阶段的技术要求。这些公司终于能够在项目的最后几年扭亏为盈，因为他们被锁定为维持服务提供商，拥有升级、维修和延长产品寿命所需的技术知识-通常在国会几乎没有兴趣或审查的情况下。
“维持费账户是一个没有人能理解的黑洞。(运维)账户是一个没人能弄清楚的黑洞，“Eaglen说。“能够改变维持的人可以改变收购游戏，这是显而易见的。”
对于空军来说，转折点是飞机机龄达到15年的时候。在这个年龄段，维护成本迅速增加，每年再增长3-7%，罗珀在9月1日的一篇文章中写道。15题为“服用红色药丸：新的数字获取现实”的文件。
但是，如果空军没有花大笔资金维修旧喷气式飞机，而是用这笔钱购买新飞机会怎么样呢？
罗珀提出的“数字世纪系列”模型不是像目前的标准那样，在几十年内大量购买一架战斗机，并将每架飞机保留30年或更长时间，而是假设先进的制造和软件开发技术使空军有可能更频繁地快速开发和购买飞机，就像20世纪50年代的情况一样，当时空军在最初的世纪系列中从六个公司购买了六架战斗机，彼此之间相距仅几年。
8月，空军先进飞机项目办公室完成了对数字世纪系列机型的商业案例分析，目的是验证这个想法在技术上是否可行，更重要的是，它是否可以节省资金。
罗珀写道，领导人发现，通过应用数字制造和开发实践-就像T-7项目以及NGAD原型开发中使用的那样-与F-35和F-15等传统战斗机相比，它可以在30年内将下一代战斗机的总寿命周期成本降低10%。
但是，空军可以每八年购买一架新战斗机，并在16年后更换它们，价格与生产生命周期为30年的数字制造战斗机的单一变种相同-在飞机达到这里的3500飞行小时大关之前，它开始需要大修和昂贵的改装来延长其使用寿命。在飞机达到3500飞行小时大关之前，空军可以每八年购买一架新战斗机，并在飞机达到3500飞行小时大关之前进行昂贵的改装，以延长其使用寿命。
他说：“我认为，制造一架而且只有一架飞机，而这架飞机必须在所有情况下始终占据所有任务的主导地位，这不是明智的想法。”“数字工程让我们可以制造不同种类的飞机，如果我们真的很聪明，…。我们确保整个机队的智能通用性--共同的支持设备、共同的驾驶舱配置、共同的接口、共同的架构，甚至像起落架这样的共同部件--简化了现场的维护和维护。“。
主要不同之处在于，空军将提前支付大部分战斗机项目成本，而不是在飞机寿命结束时支出。为了不断设计新的战斗机，该服务将不断与多家供应商签订开发新飞机的合同，大约每八年选择一次新设计。为了建立一个对工业有利可图的商业案例，它将每年购买大约50-80架飞机。
其结果是开发成本增加了25%，生产成本增加了18%。然而，罗珀在论文中写道，飞机现代化的价格将下降79%，而维持成本基本上将削减一半。
“我不能让生命周期的两端消失；工业必须在某个地方盈利，”罗珀说。“我在报纸上说，如果你可以选择用什么颜色的钱来获得未来的空中优势，那就把它用在研发和生产上，因为这是长矛的尖端，而不是今天消耗我们大量资源的老年方面。”
罗珀说，持续生产和发展战斗机也有战略上的好处。这让中国处于防御地位，必须对美国的技术进步做出回应，因为新的能力-无论是高超声速导弹还是无人机翼兵-正在成熟并螺旋式进入战斗机的生产。
他说：“这加快了我们做事的速度，这样我们就可以成为破坏者，而不是被扰乱的人，但它是以一种不能通过在问题上投入廉价劳动力而破坏的方式做到这一点的。”
下一步是空军领导层决定在22财年能够负担得起该计划的费用，以及是否会采用数字世纪系列模型来开发飞机。
他表示：“我们未来需要做的只是了解(空军)的财务承诺水平，以及他们希望我们开始行动的日期，我们就可以与(NGAD)的采购战略相适应，并解释我们能够以多快的速度承受螺旋式上升，以及我们何时需要退役飞机，以产生足够的储蓄来支付这些螺旋式下降的费用，”他表示。
“也许达到最快的(初始部署日期)可能不是最重要的事情。对我们来说，更多地突破(技术)界限可能很重要。这些都是我给领导层考虑的决定。但我给他们的每一个决定都比遗留的决定更好。“。
IRIS独立研究公司(IRIS Independent Research)的航空航天分析师丽贝卡·格兰特(Rebecca Grant)表示，如果空军要为一项要求纳税人支付更高战斗机前期成本的商业计划获得财政支持，它必须明确确定所需的作战能力。
“现在我们有了F-35、F-15EX和数字世纪系列的小批量成本，”她说。“如果它真的那么棒，或许它的前期成本是值得的。我可以肯定地说这一点。这是新的F-117吗？它的批量差不多，成本差不多，物有所值？我们只是不知道。“。
在技术方面，空军需要巩固一种严格、标准化的方法，使用建模和仿真工具在虚拟环境中进行测试活动，以减少现场飞行测试所需的时间。罗珀说，它还需要行业通过政府拥有的计算环境来接受编码。
“我们不能让每个行业伙伴都创建自己的机制，”罗珀说。我们必须拥有与物理设计组装一样严格的数码设计和组装流程。因此，我们将在政府中拥有这一点，我们将在政府中证明这一点。“。
也许最关键的是，空军将不得不向国会推销这一概念。罗珀向工作人员简要介绍了国防委员会的情况，并与许多参加这些小组的议员举行了机密会议，介绍商业案例研究的结果，以及NGAD开发和测试活动的详细进展。
“在这件事上，我有一些强硬的观众。有人告诉我想要削减这个项目，或者他们不理解我们为什么需要它，“他承认。“但我没有留下任何一次这样的简报会，除了(立法者)说：‘这是未来，我们应该现在就做。’”我们为什么不开快点呢？“。而为什么我们不能走得更快，答案很简单，那就是钱。我们今天可以把加速器往下推得更多，因为数字技术允许这样做。“
note_en:
WASHINGTON — The U.S. Air Force has secretly designed, built and flown at least one prototype of  its enigmatic next-generation fighter jet, the service’s top acquisition official confirmed to Defense News on Sept. 14.
  The development is certain to shock the defense community, which last saw the first flight of an experimental fighter during the battle for the Joint Strike Fighter contract 20 years ago. With the Air Force’s future fighter program still in its infancy, the rollout and successful first flight of a demonstrator was not expected for years.
  “We’ve already built and flown a full-scale flight demonstrator in the real world, and we broke records in doing it,” Will Roper told Defense News in an exclusive interview ahead of the  Air Force Association’s Air, Space and Cyber Conference. “We are ready to go and build the next-generation aircraft in a way that has never happened before.”
  Almost every detail about the aircraft itself will remain a mystery due to the classification of the Next Generation Air Dominance program, the Air Force’s effort for fielding a family of connected air warfare systems that could include fighters, drones and other networked platforms in space or the cyber realm.
  Roper declined to comment on how many prototype aircraft have been flown or which defense contractors manufactured them. He wouldn’t say when or where the first flight occurred. And he refused to divulge any aspect of the aircraft’s design — its mission, whether it was uncrewed or optionally crewed, whether it could fly at hypersonic speeds or if it has stealth characteristics.
      The importance, Roper said, is that just a year after the service completed an analysis of alternatives, the Air Force has proven it can use cutting-edge advanced manufacturing techniques to build and test a virtual version of its next fighter — and then move to constructing a full-scale prototype and flying it with mission systems onboard.
  “This is not just something that you can apply to things that are simple systems” like Boeing’s T-7 Red Hawk trainer jet, the first Air Force aircraft to be built using the “holy trinity” of digital engineering, agile software development and open architecture, Roper said.
             “We’re going after the most complicated systems that have ever been built, and checked all the boxes with this digital technology. In fact, [we’ve] not just checked the boxes, [we’ve] demonstrated something that’s truly magical.”
  Now, the Next Generation Air Dominance program, or NGAD, sits at a decision point. Roper declined to say how quickly the Air Force could move its next-gen fighter into production, except to say “pretty fast.” But before the service decides to begin producing a new generation of fighters, it must determine how many aircraft it will commit to buy and when it wants to start purchasing them — all choices that could influence the fiscal 2022 budget.
      The program itself has the potential to radically shake up the defense industry. Should the Air Force move to buy NGAD in the near term, it will be adding a challenger to the F-35 and F-15EX programs, potentially putting those programs at risk.
  And because the advanced manufacturing techniques that are critical for building NGAD were pioneered by the commercial sector, the program could open the door for new prime contractors for the aircraft to emerge — and perhaps  give SpaceX founder Elon Musk a shot at designing an F-35 competitor.
  “I have to imagine there will be a lot of engineers — maybe famous ones with well-known household names with billions of dollars to invest — that will decide starting the world’s greatest aircraft company to build the world’s greatest aircraft with the Air Force is exactly the kind of inspiring thing they want to do as a hobby or even a main gig,” Roper said.
  The disclosure of a flying full-scale fighter prototype could be just what the Air Force needs to garner more financial support from Congress during a critical time where the service is facing budget constraints and needs to gain momentum, said Mackenzie Eaglen, a defense budget analyst with the American Enterprise Institute.
  “If you can quickly get to something and show progress through product, it just changes the whole dynamic for the Hill,” she said. “[Roper has] got so many headwinds, it seems this would be a likely avenue to show conceptual success for his ideas.”
      Flying a prototype of its future fighter was the easy part. Now the Air Force must choose whether to commit to a radical method of buying it.
  Over the last 50 years, the U.S. industrial base has dwindled from 10 manufacturers capable of building an advanced fighter to only three defense companies: Lockheed Martin, Boeing and Northrop Grumman. The time it takes the Air Force to move a new fighter from research and development to full-rate production has stretched from a matter of years to multiple decades.
  The result is that every fighter program becomes existential for companies, who fight to prove that they can meet technical requirements during the development and production phase at a lower cost than their competitors. The companies are finally able to turn a profit during the later years of a program, when they become locked in as sustainment providers with the technical knowledge necessary for upgrading, repairing and extending the life of their product — often with little congressional interest or scrutiny.
  “The sustainment account is a black hole that nobody understands. The [operation and maintenance] account is a black hole that no one can figure out,” Eaglen said. “The person who can change sustainment can change the acquisition game, writ large.”
  For the Air Force, the turning point is when an aircraft hits 15 years old. At that age, maintenance costs compound rapidly, growing another 3-7 percent every year, Roper wrote in a Sept. 15 document titled “Take the Red Pill: The New Digital Acquisition Reality.”
    But what if instead of spending significant funds on sustaining old jets, the Air Force used that money to buy new ones?
  Instead of buying a large quantity of a single fighter over decades and retaining each plane for 30 years or more — as is currently the norm — the “Digital Century Series” model, proposed by Roper, posits that advanced manufacturing and software development techniques make it possible for the Air Force to rapidly develop and buy aircraft more frequently, much as the service did during the 1950s when it bought six fighters from six companies just years apart from each other during the original Century Series.
  In August, Air Force’s advanced aircraft program office  completed a business case analysis of the Digital Century Series model meant to validate whether the idea was technically feasible and, more importantly, whether it could save money.
  Leaders found that by applying digital manufacturing and development practices — as used by the T-7 program, as well as in the development of the NGAD prototype — it could drop the total life cycle cost of a next-gen fighter by 10 percent over 30 years compared to legacy fighters like the F-35 and F-15, Roper wrote.
    But for the same price as a single variant of a digitally manufactured fighter produced with a 30-year life cycle, the Air Force could buy a new fighter every eight years and replace them after 16 years — before the plane reaches the 3,500 flight-hour mark here it starts needing heavy overhauls and expensive modifications to extend its service life.
  “I don’t think it’s smart thinking to build one and only one aircraft that has to be dominant for all missions in all cases all the time,” he said. “Digital engineering allows us to build different kinds of airplanes, and if we’re really smart … we ensure smart commonality across the fleet — common support equipment, common cockpit configurations, common interfaces, common architecture, even common components like a landing gear — that simplify the sustainment and maintenance in the field.”
  The main difference is that the Air Force would flip from spending the majority of fighter program costs upfront instead of at the end of the aircraft’s life. To continuously design new fighter jets, the service would keep multiple vendors constantly under contract for the development of new planes, choosing a new design about every eight years. To make a business case that is profitable for industry, it would then buy batches of about 50-80 aircraft every year.
  The result is a 25 percent increase in development costs and an 18 percent increase in production costs. However, the price of modernizing aircraft would drop by 79 percent while sustainment costs are basically cut in half, Roper wrote in the paper.
  “I can’t make both ends of the life cycle go away; industry has to make a profit somewhere,” Roper said. “And I’m arguing in the paper that if you get to choose what color of money you use for future air superiority, make it research, development and production because it’s the sharp point of the spear, not the geriatric side that consumes so much of our resources today.”
  There is also a strategic benefit to continuous fighter production and development, Roper said. It puts China on the defense, having to respond to U.S. technical advances as new capabilities — whether they’re hypersonic missiles or drone wingmen — are matured and spiraled into the fighter’s production.
    “This speeds up the pace at which we can do things so that we can be the disrupter instead of the disrupted, but it does so in a way that can’t be undermined by throwing cheap labor at the problem,” he said.
  The next step is for Air Force leadership to decide how much it can afford for the program in FY22 and whether it will adopt the Digital Century Series model for developing the aircraft.
  “What we need to do going forward is simply understand the [Department of the Air Force’s] level of financial commitment and the date they want us to charge towards for initial operations, and we can fit the acquisition strategy for [NGAD] to it, and explain how quickly we can afford to spiral and when we need to retire the aircraft to generate enough savings to afford those spirals,” he said.
  “Perhaps getting to the fastest [initial fielding date] may not be the most important thing. It may be important for us to push the [technical] boundaries more. Those are decisions that I’ve given for leadership to think about. But every decision I’ve given them is a better decision over the legacy ones.”
  If the Air Force is going to get financial support for a business plan that requires taxpayers to pay a higher upfront cost for fighter aircraft, it must clearly identify desired combat capabilities, said Rebecca Grant, an aerospace analyst with IRIS Independent Research.
  “Now we have the F-35, F-15EX and the Digital Century Series' small batch costs,” she said. “If it’s that great, maybe it’s worth the upfront cost. I could argue that, for sure. Is this the new F-117, which was similar batch size at similar cost and worth every penny? We just don’t know.”
  On the technical side, the Air Force needs to solidify a rigorous, standardized method of conducting test activities in a virtual environment using  modeling and simulation tools that can cut down the amount of time needed for live flight tests. It also needs industry to buy in to coding via a government-owned computing environment, Roper said.
  “We can’t have every industry partner creating their own mechanism,” Roper said. “We have to have just as rigorous a process for digital design and assembly as we do for physical design assembly. So we will own that in the government, we will certify that in the government.”
  And — perhaps most critically — the Air Force will have to sell the concept to Congress. Roper has briefed staff members on the defense committees, and he held classified sessions with many of the lawmakers who sit on those panels to present findings of the business case study as well as the detailed progress of NGAD development and test activities.
  “I had some tough audiences on this. I’ve had people that I’ve been told want to cut the program or they don’t understand why we need it,” he acknowledged. “But I have not left a single one of those briefings with anything other than [lawmakers saying]: ‘This is the future, we ought to do it now. And why aren’t we going faster?’ And the answer [to] why we aren’t going faster is simply money. We can push the accelerator down more today because the digital technology allows it.”
title: 美国空军制造并试飞了未来战斗机的原型
title_en: The US Air Force has built and flown a prototype of its future fighter jet

note:
微软扩大了与总部位于旧金山的人工智能研究公司OpenAI的持续合作伙伴关系，获得了这家人工智能公司突破性的GPT-3语言模型的新独家许可。GPT-3语言模型是一个自动生成文本的程序，已成为业内同类程序中最复杂的。
两家公司已经通过OpenAI正在进行的Azure云计算合同进行了交织，Azure是OpenAI访问培训许多模型所需的大量计算资源的平台，微软去年进行了10亿美元的重大投资，以成为OpenAI的独家云提供商。现在，微软通过获得GPT-3的版权，再次发出了对OpenAI研究高度信任的信号。
OpenAI在7月份发布了GPT-3，这是其不断增长的语言模型的第三次迭代，该程序及其之前的迭代帮助创建了一些迄今为止最吸引人的人工智能语言实验。这也引发了围绕强大的人工智能程序伦理的激烈辩论，这些程序可能被用于更邪恶的目的，OpenAI最初拒绝发表关于该模型的研究，因为担心它会被滥用。
“该交易对继续通过OpenAI的API获取GPT-3型号没有影响。”
不像大多数人工智能系统是为一种用例而设计的，OpenAI的API今天提供了一个通用的‘文本输入，文本输出’界面，允许用户在几乎任何英语任务上试用它。GPT-3是当今API背后最强大的模型，有1750亿个参数，“OpenAI在一篇关于其与微软合作的博客文章中解释道。
微软首席技术官凯文·斯科特(Kevin Scott)在宣布这笔交易的公司博客文章中写道：“我们认为这是一个令人难以置信的机会，可以扩大我们基于Azure的人工智能平台，使人工智能技术民主化，实现新的产品、服务和体验，并大规模增加人工智能的积极影响。”“我们在微软的使命是让地球上的每个人和每个组织都能取得更多成就，所以我们希望确保这个人工智能平台对每个人都是可用的-研究人员、企业家、业余爱好者和企业-以增强他们创造新事物和有趣事物的雄心。”
OpenAI表示，“这笔交易对继续通过OpenAI的API访问GPT-3模型没有任何影响，它的现有用户和未来用户将继续像往常一样使用我们的API构建应用程序。”这确实引发了一些有趣的问题，即微软到底在这里获得了什么。微软的一位发言人告诉The Verge，其独家许可证使其能够独一无二地访问GPT-3的底层代码，其中包含它希望整合到其产品和服务中的技术进步。
因此，虽然其他公司和研究人员可能能够通过OpenAI的API访问GPT-3，但只有微软将获得利用所有人工智能进步使其成为如此复杂的程序的好处。目前还不清楚这会是什么样子，但可以肯定的是，微软从OpenAI的工作中看到了巨大的价值，很可能希望成为第一家(在这种情况下，也是唯一家)将其主要是实验性研究工作转化为现实产品进步的公司。
note_en:
Microsoft has expanded its ongoing partnership with San Francisco-based artificial intelligence research company OpenAI with a  new exclusive license on the AI firm’s groundbreaking GPT-3 language model, an auto-generating text program that’s emerged as the  most sophisticated of its kind in the industry.
 The two companies are already entwined through OpenAI’s  ongoing Azure cloud computing contract, with Azure being the platform on which OpenAI accesses the vast computing resources it needs to train many of its models, and a  major $1 billion investment Microsoft made last year to become OpenAI’s exclusive cloud provider. Now, Microsoft is issuing yet another signal of high confidence in OpenAI’s research by acquiring the rights to GPT-3.
 OpenAI released GPT-3, the third iteration of its ever-growing language model, in July, and the program and its prior iterations have helped create some of the most fascinating AI language experiments to date. It’s also inspired vigorous debate around the  ethics of powerful AI programs that may be used for more nefarious purposes, with OpenAI  initially refusing to publish research about the model for fear it would be misused.
 “The deal has no impact on continued access to the GPT-3 model through OpenAI’s API.”
 “Unlike most AI systems which are designed for one use-case, OpenAI’s API today provides a general-purpose ‘text in, text out’ interface, allowing users to try it on virtually any English language task. GPT-3 is the most powerful model behind the API today, with 175 billion parameters,” OpenAI explains in a  blog post about its partnership with Microsoft.
 “We see this as an incredible opportunity to expand our Azure-powered AI platform in a way that democratizes AI technology, enables new products, services and experiences, and increases the positive impact of AI at Scale,” writes Microsoft chief technology officer Kevin Scott in the company’s  blog post announcing the deal. “Our mission at Microsoft is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more, so we want to make sure that this AI platform is available to everyone – researchers, entrepreneurs, hobbyists, businesses – to empower their ambitions to create something new and interesting.”
 OpenAI says, “the deal has no impact on continued access to the GPT-3 model through OpenAI’s API, and existing and future users of it will continue building applications with our API as usual,” which does raise some interesting questions about what exactly Microsoft has acquired here. A Microsoft spokesperson tells  The Verge that its exclusive license gives it unique access to the underlying code of GPT-3, which contains technical advancements it hopes to integrate into its products and services.
 So while other companies and researchers may be able to access GPT-3 through OpenAI’s API, only Microsoft will reap the benefits of getting to make use of all the AI advancements that went into making it such a sophisticated program. It’s not clear what that will look like right now, but what is clear is that Microsoft sees immense value in OpenAI’s work and likely wants to be the first (and, in this case, only) company to take its largely experimental research work and translate it into real-world product advancements.
title: 微软独家授权OpenAI的GPT-3文本生成模型
title_en: Microsoft exclusively licenses OpenAI’s GPT-3 text generation model

note:
特斯拉在电池日并没有退缩，宣布了一款新的台式4680电池外形规格，以及其他许多东西。新的外形规格消除了突片，提高了能量密度，保持了与较小电池相似的热特性，提高了功率重量比，简化了制造流程，降低了成本。
换句话说，新的特斯拉4680电池相当于带着一把土豆枪去打薯条。让我们详细介绍一下新外形带来的每一项改进。
特斯拉满足于用其专有化学物质从供应商那里购买电池，但看到了地平线上的问题。特斯拉的目标是以每年40-50%的速度持续增长，要做到这一点，它将需要越来越多的电池。特斯拉的电池预测显示，其电池供应商的限产与特斯拉对汽车和储能业务的内部需求之间存在差距。
为了解决这个问题，特斯拉的团队一直在努力购买和设计新的技术解决方案，以推动电池格式的进一步改进。或许更重要的是，特斯拉利用其在电池制造领域的专业知识以及世界领先的制造设备能力，从头到尾重新思考了整个过程。
我们打算增加而不是减少从松下、LG&amp；CATL(可能还有其他合作伙伴)购买电池。然而，即使我们的电池供应商开足马力，我们仍预计2022年及以后会出现严重短缺，除非我们自己也采取行动。
随着特斯拉在汽车制造领域寻求更高的效率，它很快就超越了单纯的设计和制造产品，进入了制造机器的机器的设计领域。制造很难，但特斯拉的承诺让它超越了产品，设计了更容易制造的产品。我们在Model3中第一次看到了这一点，并且工作在每一次迭代中都在继续。
到了设计新电池的时候，特斯拉一直往上看，一直到起点。这样做反映了马斯克对首要原则思维的痴迷。他试图以真正最低的公分母开始每一项设计、每一次讨论-这是将我们的产品锚定在它们所组成的矿物上的最简单的事实。当这些都经过反复定义和改进后，就可以构建优化设计了。
note_en:
Tesla didn’t hold back at Battery Day, announcing a new tabless 4680 cell form factor, among many other things. The new form factor eliminates the tabs, increases energy density, maintains similar thermal characteristics of smaller cells, improves the power-to-weight ratio, streamlines manufacturing, and lowers cost.
 Said another way, the new Tesla 4680 cell is the equivalent of bringing a potato gun to a french fry fight. Let’s walk through each of the improvements the new form factor brings to the table.
  Tesla was content buying cells with its proprietary chemistry from its suppliers, but saw issues on the horizon. Tesla aims to grow consistently at a rate of 40–50% per year, and to do that, it is going to need more and more batteries. Tesla’s battery forecasts showed a gap between the production limits of its battery cell suppliers and Tesla’s internal demand for its automotive and energy storage businesses.
  To solve this, Tesla’s teams have been hard at work buying and designing new technical solutions to drive further improvements in the battery cell format. Perhaps more importantly, Tesla leveraged its know-how in the battery cell manufacturing space along with its world-leading manufacturing equipment competency to rethink the entire process from start to finish.
 We intend to increase, not reduce battery cell purchases from Panasonic, LG & CATL (possibly other partners too). However, even with our cell suppliers going at maximum speed, we still foresee significant shortages in 2022 & beyond unless we also take action ourselves.
   As it sought greater efficiencies in automotive manufacturing, Tesla quickly pushed beyond merely designing and building products, going into the design of  the machine that builds the machine. Manufacturing is hard, but Tesla’s commitment led it beyond the product to designing products that were easier to manufacturer. We first saw this with the Model 3, and work has continued with every iteration.
 When the time came to design the new battery, Tesla looked all the way up the chain, to the starting point. Doing this is a reflection of Musk’s obsession with first principles thinking. He tries to start every design, every discussion with what is truly the lowest common denominator — the simplest truth anchoring our products to the minerals they are comprised of. When those have been defined and refined over and over again, it is then possible to build an optimized design.
title: 关于特斯拉新款4680电池，你需要知道的一切
title_en: Everything You Need to Know About Tesla’s New 4680 Battery Cell

note:
吉卜力制片厂发布了其8部经典电影中400张免费使用的图片的宝库。这些照片可以在制片厂的网站上找到，来自“在罂粟山上”、“波妞”、“千与千寻”、“卡古亚公主的故事”、“阿丽埃蒂的秘密世界”、“玛妮在那里时的地海故事”和“起风了”。吉卜力制片厂表示，随着时间的推移，他们将发布所有电影的画面。
“从这个月开始，我们将按顺序提供所有吉卜力工作室作品的场景照片，”吉卜力工作室网站顶部的一条谷歌翻译的消息写道。“本月，我们将提供8件作品，以新作为主，共计400件。在常识范围内随意使用。“。
吉卜力电影以其令人难以置信的艺术而闻名，所以这个收藏是近距离观看电影剧照的一个非常酷的方式。我可以花几个小时钻研所有这些东西。由于它们可以免费使用，它们可以做成很棒的桌面壁纸，甚至可以作为您定制的iOS14主屏幕的背景。
如果你是Ghibli的粉丝，这一年对你来说是很好的一年-除了这些令人敬畏的画廊(以及4月份发布的免费缩放背景)，当HBO Max在5月份推出这项服务时，制片厂目录中的电影都在HBO Max上热映，2月份有21部Ghibli电影出现在Netflix上，出现在美国、加拿大和日本以外的地区。我真的推荐花一些时间看吉卜力工作室的电影，如果你需要一个指南来指导从哪里开始，我们可以帮你解决。
note_en:
Studio Ghibli has released a treasure trove of 400 free-to-use images from eight of its classic movies. The images, which are available on the  studio’s website, come from  From Up On Poppy Hill,  Ponyo,  Spirited Away,  The Tale of The Princess Kaguya,  The Secret World of Arrietty,  Tales From Earthsea,  When Marnie Was There, and  The Wind Rises. Studio Ghibli says it will release images from all of its films over time.
 “From this month, we will provide scene photos of all Studio Ghibli works in sequence,” reads a Google-translated message at the top of the  Studio Ghibli website. “This month, we will provide 8 works, mainly new works, for a total of 400 pieces. Feel free to use it within the bounds of common sense.”
 Ghibli movies are renowned for their incredible art, so this collection is a really cool way to look at stills from the films up close. I could spend hours poring through all of these. And since they’re free to use, they could make for great desktop wallpapers or maybe even the background of your  customized iOS 14 home screen.
 If you’re a Ghibli fan, it’s been a good year for you — in addition to these awesome galleries (and the free Zoom backgrounds released  in April), movies from the studio’s catalog hit HBO Max when the service launched  in May, and 21 Ghibli films appeared on Netflix in regions outside the US, Canada, and Japan  in February. I really recommend spending some time with Studio Ghibli’s movies, and if you need a guide on where to start,  we’ve got you covered.
title: 吉卜力制片厂发布了其八部经典电影中的400张免费使用的图片
title_en: Studio Ghibli releases 400 free-to-use images from eight of its classic films

note:
特斯拉首席执行官承认，新电池的设计和制造过程还没有完成，他说这将是成本的一半。
埃隆·马斯克(Elon Musk)在周二的特斯拉(Tesla)“电池日”上描述了新一代电动汽车电池，这种电池将比公司目前的电池更强大、更持久，价格只有目前的一半。
马斯克说，特斯拉新的更大的圆柱形电池将提供五倍的能量，六倍的功率和16%的行驶里程，并补充说，全面生产大约需要三年时间。
“我们没有一辆负担得起的车。这是我们未来将拥有的东西。但我们必须降低电池成本，“马斯克说。
马斯克说，为了帮助降低成本，特斯拉计划在内华达州的“巨型工厂”回收电池，同时将钴--最昂贵的电池材料之一--降至几乎为零。该公司还计划在世界各地的几家高度自动化的工厂生产自己的电池。
据特斯拉负责动力总成和能源工程的高级副总裁德鲁·巴格利诺(Drew Baglino)表示，这家汽车制造商计划通过高度自动化、连续运动的组装过程生产新电池。
在活动上发表讲话时，马斯克概述了削减成本的计划，这位首席执行官承认，特斯拉的新电池设计和制造流程尚未完全完成。
这家汽车制造商的股价下跌，因为马斯克预测这一变化可能需要三年时间。特斯拉经常未能实现生产目标。
特斯拉预计最终每年能生产多达2000万辆电动汽车。今年，整个汽车业预计将在全球交付8000万辆汽车。
在吸引了超过27万在线观众的活动开幕式上，马斯克走上舞台，约240名股东-每人坐在公司停车场的特斯拉Model 3-按喇叭表示赞同。
随着汽车制造商从马力转向千瓦，以遵守更严格的环境法规，投资者正在寻找证据，证明特斯拉可以增加其在电气化技术方面的领先地位，而传统汽车制造商的大部分销售和利润来自内燃机汽车。
尽管近年来由于电池组成的变化，电动汽车的平均价格有所下降，但它们仍然比传统汽车更贵，据估计，电池占电动汽车成本的四分之一到三分之一。
一些研究人员估计，当电池组的价格为每千瓦时100美元时，就会达到价格平价，也就是电动汽车与内燃车的价值相等。
根据电动汽车咨询公司凯恩能源研究顾问公司(凯恩Energy Research Advisors)的数据，2019年特斯拉的电池组价格为每千瓦时156美元，这将使90千瓦时电池组的成本约为1.4万美元。
除了弗里蒙特的新工厂外，特斯拉还在德国的新工厂建设自己的电池制造设施。
note_en:
Tesla CEO acknowledged the design and manufacturing process of the new cells, which he says will be half as expensive, is not complete
    Elon Musk described a new generation of electric vehicle batteries that will be more powerful, longer lasting, and half as expensive as the company’s current cells at Tesla’s “Battery Day” on Tuesday.
 Tesla’s new larger cylindrical cells will provide five times more energy, six times more power and 16% greater driving range, Musk said, adding that full production is about three years away.
  “We do not have an affordable car. That’s something we will have in the future. But we’ve got to get the cost of batteries down,” Musk said.
  To help reduce cost, Musk said Tesla planned to recycle battery cells at its Nevada “gigafactory,” while reducing cobalt – one of the most expensive battery materials – to virtually zero. It also plans to manufacture its own battery cells at several highly automated factories around the world.
 The automaker plans to produce the new cells via a highly automated, continuous-motion assembly process, according to Drew Baglino, Tesla senior vice-president of powertrain and energy engineering.
 Speaking at the event, during which Musk outlined plans to cut costs, the CEO acknowledged that Tesla does not have its new battery design and manufacturing process fully complete.
 The automaker’s shares slipped as Musk forecast the change could take three years. Tesla has frequently missed production targets.
 Tesla expects to eventually be able to build as many as 20m electric vehicles a year. This year, the entire auto industry expects to deliver 80m cars globally.
 At the opening of the event, which drew over 270,000 online viewers, Musk walked on stage as about 240 shareholders – each sitting in a Tesla Model 3 in the company parking lot – honked their car horns in approval.
 As automakers shift from horsepower to kilowatts to comply with stricter environmental regulations, investors are looking for evidence that Tesla can increase its lead in electrification technology over legacy automakers who generate most of their sales and profits from combustion-engine vehicles.
 While average electric vehicle prices have decreased in recent years thanks to changes in battery composition, they are still more expensive than conventional cars, with the battery estimated to make up a quarter to a third of an electric vehicle’s cost.
 Some researchers estimate that price parity, or the point at which electric vehicles are equal in value to internal combustion cars, is reached when battery packs cost $100 per kilowatt hour (kWh).
 Tesla’s battery packs cost $156 per kWh in 2019, according to electric vehicle consulting firm Cairn Energy Research Advisors, which would put the cost of a 90-kWh pack at around $14,000.
 Tesla is also building its own cell manufacturing facility at its new factory in Germany in addition to the new plant in Fremont.

note:
近两个月来，印度极右翼印度教民族主义者的推文在Twitter上流传，抨击数十对年轻的跨宗教夫妇-通常是穆斯林男性与印度女性结婚。
“这将是一条很长的线，”参与推特活动的一个账号说，随后又发了17条推文。每条推文都包含政府文件的照片，包括姓名、年龄、职业、地址和印度教-穆斯林夫妇在印度的照片。“看看这些照片，”来自同一账号的另一条推文说。“是谁怂恿这些情侣走到一起的？他们不可能只是“坠入爱河”。“。
周一，随着印度民众的愤怒情绪日益高涨，Twitter终于撤下了一些最大的帖子，尽管人们几周来一直在报道这些帖子。
但在第一次被删除后，其他六条支持跨宗教伴侣的推文仍然存在。其中一条推文来自印度执政党印度人民党(Bharatiya Janata Party)的一名政客，他在推特上发布了一名据称皈依伊斯兰教的印度演员的地址。在BuzzFeed新闻询问这些帖子后，Twitter删除了这些帖子。
推特的一位发言人告诉BuzzFeed新闻，“未经同意发布某人的私人信息是直接违反推特规则的，任何人都不能参与有针对性的骚扰某人，或煽动其他人这样做。”
在推特增长最快的市场之一印度，备受争议的特别婚姻法要求不同信仰的情侣向政府宣布结婚意向，并等待30天的批准，在此期间，这对情侣的申请将在婚姻登记部门接受公众审查。这项法律目前正在印度最高法院受到挑战，一名请愿人称其为“不公正、非法和违宪”。
但尽管存在争议，去年喀拉拉邦走得更远：它将所有申请上传到婚姻登记网站，任何人都可以在那里下载。
这一举措在社交媒体上掀起了数百份跨信仰婚姻申请。印度教民族主义者声称，这些申请是“爱情圣战”的证据，这是一种毫无根据的极右翼阴谋论，指责穆斯林男子与印度教女性结婚，以引诱她们背离自己的信仰，慢慢地将印度这个印度教占多数的国家变成一个伊斯兰国家。这些阴谋论助长了暴力，与此同时，印度总理纳伦德拉·莫迪(Narendra Modi)利用印度教民族主义为自己的权力崛起提供了动力。
两对申请发布在该平台上的夫妇告诉BuzzFeed新闻，尽管Facebook过去曾因让与“爱情圣战”相关的仇恨言论在其平台上蓬勃发展而受到批评，但每当这些特定的Doxing事件被报道时，该公司确实会将它们删除。但这些文件充满了个人和身份信息，在Twitter上自由漂浮了几个月。
其中一些推文获得了数千条转发和点赞，并给印度极右翼的知名成员贴上了标签，其中包括印度人民党政客卡皮尔·米什拉(Kapil Mishra)，他在2月份的一次集会上的演讲被上传到Facebook上，据信在德里引发了宗教暴力，导致50多人死亡，其中大多数是穆斯林。
7月份的一条推文写道：“印度教血统正在被灭绝；大规模对话正在进行中。”这条推文被转发了近3000次。
另一条推文写道：“我会发布我的每一份[申请]。”这条推文被转发了近500次。
“总共有72对夫妇。这是一场消灭印度教徒的[运动]，“另一位读者写道，标签为#印度教徒受到攻击。
推特说：“我们明智而公正地为每个人执行我们的政策。”“我们的产品和政策从来不是在政治意识形态的基础上制定和实施的。如果人们在推特上看到违反推特规则的事情，他们能做的最重要的事情就是举报，方法是点击推文顶部的下拉箭头，然后选择报告推文。
总部位于加州的科技公司内外的批评者都声称，这些平台未能对抗全球危害。正如一名最近被解雇的Facebook员工在一份内部备忘录中抱怨的那样，“我们专注于危害和优先考虑的地区，如美国和西欧。”
班加罗尔的一位公共政策专业人士Athera Sujatha Radhakrishnan在接受BuzzFeed新闻采访时表示：“爱情圣战并不是一场全球性的运动，但现在是时候让这些西方公司了解这样的事情在他们地理位置之外的现实世界的影响了。”
去年，印度教徒Radhakrishnan和她的穆斯林丈夫被处决。他们的跨宗教婚姻申请是印度教民族主义者从喀拉拉邦政府网站下载的150个应用程序之一。他们的应用程序没有发布在Twitter上，但在通过邻居联系到她母亲之前，它仍然在WhatsApp群中传播，并附上了一条信息，要求她“停止爱情圣战”。
Radhakrishnan向警方提出申诉，并在今年早些时候写了一篇Facebook帖子，在帖子中她标记了负责婚姻登记的部长。今年7月，该部撤回了将跨宗教婚姻申请上传到其网站的决定。
拉达克里希南说，她计划本周晚些时候向最高法院提交自己的请愿书，重点关注这项法律可能侵犯隐私的问题。
周二，一名推特用户写道：“如果这种情况发生在美国，异族情侣被拒绝，我相信会迅速采取行动。”“但谁会在乎一群印度人呢？他们的死亡不会让国际媒体报道，我是说每天都有成群结队的人死在这里，阿米利特？“
note_en:
For nearly two months, tweets by far-right Hindu nationalists in India doxing dozens of young interfaith couples — usually Muslim men marrying Hindu women — circulated on Twitter.
 “This is going to be a long thread,” one of the accounts involved in the doxing said, following it up with 17 more tweets. Each tweet contained pictures of government documents including names, ages, occupations, addresses, and photographs of Hindu-Muslim couples in India. “Look at these pictures,” another tweet from the same account said. “Who instigates these couples to get together? It can’t be that they just ‘fall in love.’”
 On Monday, as outrage mounted in India, Twitter finally took down some of the largest threads, even though people had been reporting them for weeks.
 But more than half a dozen other tweets doxing interfaith couples remained after the first takedowns. One of them included a tweet from a politician from India’s ruling Bharatiya Janata Party, who tweeted the address of an Indian actor who allegedly converted to Islam. Twitter took down these posts after BuzzFeed News asked about them.
  A Twitter spokesperson told BuzzFeed News, “Posting a person's private information without their consent is a direct violation of the Twitter Rules and one may not engage in the targeted harassment of someone, or incite other people to do so.”
 In India, one of Twitter’s fastest-growing markets, the  controversial Special Marriage Act requires interfaith couples to announce their intent to get married to the government and wait 30 days for approval, during which the couple’s application is put up for public scrutiny at the marriage registration department. The law is currently being challenged in the Indian Supreme Court, with a petitioner  calling it “unjust, illegal, and unconstitutional.”
 But despite the controversies, last year, the state of Kerala went a step further: It uploaded all applications to its marriage registration website, where anyone could download them.
 The move swept hundreds of interfaith marriage applications onto social media. Hindu nationalists claimed the applications were proof of “love jihad,” a baseless far-right conspiracy theory that  accuses Muslim men of marrying Hindu women to lure them away from their faith, slowly turning India, a Hindu-majority country, into an Islamic nation. Those conspiracy theories have fueled  violence at the same time that Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi has tapped into Hindu nationalism to fuel his rise to power.
 Although Facebook has been  criticized for letting “love jihad”–related hate speech flourish on its platform in the past, the company did take down these particular instances of doxing whenever they were reported, two couples whose applications were posted to the platform told BuzzFeed News. But the documents, full of personal and identifying information, floated freely on Twitter for months.
 Some of these tweets had thousands of retweets and likes and tagged prominent members of the Indian far right, including Kapil Mishra, a BJP politician whose speech at a February rally was uploaded to Facebook and  is believed to have sparked religious violence that killed more than 50 people in the state of Delhi — most of them Muslims.
 “Hindu bloodlines are being exterminated; mass conversations in progress,” read a tweet from July that had nearly 3,000 retweets.
 “I will post each [application] I have,” read another tweet with nearly 500 retweets.
 “72 couples in total. This is a [campaign] to obliterate Hindus,” another read, with the hashtag #Hindus_Under_Attack.
 “We enforce our policies judiciously and impartially for everyone,” Twitter said. “Our product and policies are never developed or implemented on the basis of political ideology. If people on Twitter see something that violates the Twitter Rules, the most important thing they can do is report it, by clicking the drop down arrow at the top of the Tweet and selecting Report Tweet.”
 Critics both inside and outside the technology companies headquartered in California allege that the platforms have failed to combat global harm. As a recently fired Facebook employee complained in an internal memo, “ We focus upon harm and priority regions like the United States and Western Europe.”
 “Love jihad is not a global campaign, but it’s high time for these Western companies to understand the real-world impact of something like this outside their geographies,” Athira Sujatha Radhakrishnan, a public policy professional in Bangalore, told BuzzFeed News.
 Last year, Radhakrishnan, who is Hindu, and her husband, who is Muslim, were doxed. Their interfaith marriage application was one of the 150 applications that Hindu nationalists downloaded from the Kerala government’s website. Their application wasn’t posted to Twitter, but it still made its way through WhatsApp groups before reaching her mother through a neighbor, accompanied by a message to “stop love jihad.”
 Radhakrishnan filed a police complaint and earlier this year wrote a Facebook post in which she tagged the minister in charge of marriage registrations. In July, the ministry  walked back its decision to upload interfaith marriage applications to its website.
 Radhakrishnan said she planned to file her own petition in the Supreme Court later this week focusing on the law's potential to violate privacy.
  “If this happened in America with interracial couples being doxed, there would be rapid action, I am sure of it,” one Twitter user  wrote on Tuesday. “But who cares about a bunch of Indian people? It’s not like their dying will make the international press, I mean people die here in the droves every day, amirite?”

苹果周三在印度推出了在线商店，除了提供几乎整个系列的产品外，还首次为该国消费者带来了一系列服务。印度是苹果推出在线商店的第38个市场。
印度的消费者现在可以购买AppleCare+，它延长了产品的保修期限，并可以访问以旧换新计划，以便在购买新硬件时获得折扣。该公司表示，它还将通过聊天或电话为客户提供支持，并让用户在购买之前咨询其专家团队。该公司还允许客户订购定制版本的iMac、MacBook Air、Mac Mini和其他Mac电脑-5月下旬，该公司通过印度的授权合作伙伴开始离线订购iMac、MacBook Air、Mac Mini和其他Mac电脑。
该公司还向客户提供按月分期付款的能力。TechCrunch在1月份报道称，该公司计划在截至9月份的季度在印度开设在线商店。该公司表示，计划明年在中国开设第一家实体零售店。
咨询公司Conversion Catalyst的首席分析师贾扬斯·科拉(Jayanth Kolla)认为，苹果在线商店在印度的推出对公司来说比该国的消费者更重要。
他告诉TechCrunch，苹果通常只有在市场开设门店后才开始在市场上投资营销、品牌建设和其他投资。
科拉说，苹果确实监督在印度展示的iPhone和其他产品的广告牌和广告，但运营这些产品并为其提供资金的是第三方合作伙伴。“苹果可能会提供一些营销资金，但这些努力总是由他们的合作伙伴领导的，”他说。
近年来，苹果对印度的兴趣明显增加，印度是世界上增长最快的智能手机市场之一。该公司的代工制造商今天在当地组装最新一代的iPhone型号和一些配件-该公司两年前就开始了这一努力。
此举使苹果公司能够在印度降低一些iPhone机型的价格，多年来，该公司一直在印度将关税费用转嫁给客户。IPhone11 Pro Max在印度的起价为1,487美元，而在美国为1,099美元。AirPods Pro在美国的售价为249美元，在印度上市时的售价为341美元。
科拉说，与大多数在印度免费提供产品和服务或以世界上一些最便宜的价格提供产品和服务的外国公司不同，苹果完全专注于一小部分有能力支付大笔费用的人口。科拉认为，这一战略对公司来说效果很好。
这并不是说苹果没有对其价格策略做出一些改变。Apple Music在印度的月费为1.35美元，而在美国为9.99美元。
一些苹果客户表示，尽管他们更喜欢这家iPhone制造商的产品生态系统，而不是Android制造商的产品，但他们希望苹果在印度提供更多服务。“包括苹果新闻(Apple News)和苹果支付(Apple Pay)在内的一系列苹果服务在印度仍然不可用。
note_en:
Apple launched its  online store in India on Wednesday, which in addition to offering nearly the entire line-up of its products, also brings a range of services for the first time to consumers in the country. India is the 38th market for Apple where it has launched its online store.
 Consumers in India can now purchase AppleCare+, which extends warranty on products, and access the trade-in program to get a discount on new hardware purchases. The company said it will also offer customers support through chat or telephone, and let users consult its team of specialists before they make a purchase. The company is also letting customers order customized versions of iMac, MacBook Air, Mac Mini and other Mac computers — something it  started offline through its authorized partners in late May in India.
  The company is also offering customers the ability to pay for their purchases in monthly instalments. TechCrunch reported in January that the company was planning to open its online store in India in the  quarter that ends in September. The company plans to open its  first physical retail store in the country next year, it has said.
 Jayanth Kolla, chief analyst at consultancy firm Convergence Catalyst, argued that the launch of the Apple’s online store in India is a bigger deal for the company than the consumers in the country.
 Apple typically starts investing in marketing, brand building and other investments in a market only after it launches a store there, he told TechCrunch.
 Apple does oversee billboards and ads of iPhones and other products that are displayed in India, but it’s the third-party partners that are running and bankrolling them, said Kolla. “Apple might provide some marketing dollars, but those efforts are always led by their partners,” he said.
 In recent years, Apple has visibly grown more interested in India, one of the world’s fastest growing smartphones markets. The company’s contract manufacturers today locally  assemble the latest generation of iPhone models and some accessories — an effort the company kickstarted two years ago.
 The move has allowed Apple to lower prices of some iPhone models in India, where for years the company has passed custom duty charges to customers. The starting price of iPhone 11 Pro Max is $1,487 in India, compared to $1,099 in the U.S. The AirPods Pro, which sells at $249 in the U.S., was made available in India at $341 at the time of launch.
 Unlike most foreign firms that offer their products and services for free in India or at some of the world’s cheapest prices, Apple has focused entirely on a small fraction of the population that can afford to pay big bucks, Kolla said. And that strategy has worked fine for the company, Kolla argued.
 That’s not to say that Apple has not made some changes to its price strategy. The monthly cost of Apple Music is $1.35 in India, compared to $9.99 in the U.S.
 Some Apple customers say even as they prefer the iPhone-maker’s ecosystem of products over Android makers’ offerings, they wish Apple made more of its services available in the country. A range of Apple services including Apple News and Apple Pay are still not available in India.
